title: Suja Life Sees 66% EBITDA Growth as Functional Beverage Boom Accelerates
content:
By Karen Roman

Suja Life, Inc. (Nasdaq: SUJA) said first quarter net sales grew 22.5% to $107.1 million compared to $87.4 million the year prior, while gross profit increased 24.3% to $54.1 million, or 50.5% of net sales, compared to $43.5 million, or 49.8% of net sales.

Net income rose to $7.7 million compared to a net loss of $0.8 million the previous year, and adjusted EBITDA increased 66.3% to $25 million compared to $15 million, with adjusted EBITDA margins of 23.4% vs 17.2%, it stated.

Never Miss our Weekly Highlights HERE

“Our performance reflects the strength of our category-leading brands and our vertically integrated platform,” said Maria Stipp, Suja Life CEO. “As a newly public company, we are building on our established track record of profitable growth and are well-positioned for long-term success.”

For 2026 it expects net sales between $367 to $371 million, up from $326.6 million in 2025, and adjusted EBITDA between $70 to $72 million, up from $40.5 million the previous year, the company said.

READ MORE

Highlights: Tech Edge at Pax8 Beyond 2026 in Salt Lake City

Contact:

Exec Edge

Editor@executives-edge.com

Click HERE to follow us on LinkedIn

The post Suja Life Sees 66% EBITDA Growth as Functional Beverage Boom Accelerates appeared first on ExecEdge.

title: Goldman Sachs Says Buy the Dip in These 2 Newly Public ‘Strong Buy’ Stocks
content:
The IPO market is showing signs of life again. Despite a challenging geopolitical backdrop and lingering uncertainty across global markets, companies continue to make their way to the public markets, with more than 30 IPOs completed so far this year. Meanwhile, investors are eagerly awaiting the potential debuts of high-profile companies such as SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools. Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Against that backdrop, Goldman Sachs Chief U.S. Equity Strategist Ben Snider sees reason for cautious optimism. While several indicators of investor exuberance are running above historical averages, Snider notes they remain well below the extremes seen during the market peaks of 2000 and 2021.

That constructive view extends to the IPO market, with Snider noting: “The number of IPOs this year is on track to match the long-term annual average of 100. However, activity is increasing, and we expect a record year for the dollar value of US equity issuance in 2026. Relative to equity market cap, however, issuance should register close to the average volume from 2015-2019, and we expect corporate equity demand will continue to outweigh supply this year.”

Goldman Sachs’ stock analysts are also taking notice of the IPO market, highlighting two newly public companies that have fallen at least 18% since their debuts and now appear to offer attractive entry points.

According to the TipRanks database, both names have also garnered Strong Buy consensus ratings from the broader analyst community. Let’s take a closer look.

HawkEye 360(HAWK)

The first Goldman pick we’ll look at here is HawkEye 360, a company at the leading edge of the global market for signals intelligence and data analytics. Specifically, HawkEye 360 works with the defense and intelligence industries, providing the technology necessary to support mission-critical operations. The company’s specialty is the use of satellites to collect and analyze radio frequency signal location data.

HawkEye 360’s satellite constellation allows for global data collection. The company has a proprietary signal processing system, and uses AI to power its analysis. The system has been proven successful at the operational level, bringing trustworthy domain awareness and early warning data to US government and military agencies, and their allies around the world.

Looking at the IPO, which was held last month, we see that HawkEye priced its offering on May 6 at $26 per share. HAWK shares started trading the next day, and spiked to $34 per share. The company put 16 million shares of common stock on the market, and raised some $416 million in gross proceeds from the event.

Story Continues

Since then, however, the shares have fallen 24% from their post-IPO highs as enthusiasm surrounding the debut cooled and investors reassessed valuations across the sector.

That pullback has created an opportunity to take a closer look at the company’s longer-term prospects, and Goldman Sachs analyst Noah Poponak believes the underlying story remains compelling.

“HawkEye is one of the only commercial providers of unclassified, RF-based SIGINT operating in low earth orbit (LEO) today, lending scarcity value to its asset base and signals processing capabilities. We expect demand for HawkEye’s services to grow both domestically and internationally, and there is upside to estimates if the company gains commercial traction. Operating on a relatively fixed cost base, HawkEye has the potential to grow margins and FCF generation substantially as the company scales. In addition to operating leverage, the next generation of satellites (Block 3) are ~75% less capital intensive to build and launch, which will help drive better margins and FCF generation,” Poponak opined.

Getting to specifics about the company’s prospects, Poponak writes, “At 11.5X CY27 EV/Sales, HawkEye screens attractively against a broad space comp group that trades at 14.9X on average. We think a higher multiple is warranted given the company’s relatively small size today (which will help with the pace of revenue growth), and better than average profitability and out-year FCF generation.”

These comments back up the Goldman analyst’s Buy rating here, while his $42 price target implies a sound one-year gain for the stock of ~60%. (To watch Poponak’s track record, click here)

Overall, the 8 recent analyst reviews here split 6 to 2 in favor of Buys over Holds, giving HAWK its Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are currently trading for $26.29 and their $38.86 average target price suggests an upside of ~48% for the coming year. (See HAWK stock forecast)

Suja Life(SUJA)

Next up is Suja Life, a California-based fruit juice company that targets the health-conscious market. Suja Life’s market research has led the company to several conclusions: that consumers want beverages that are healthy, that taste good, and that have less sugar. To that end, Suja Life has put together three brands, to target different dynamics of these consumer needs.

First is Suja Organic, the company’s leading brand of cold-pressed juices. These juices are packed with essential vitamins and minerals, as well as superfoods and probiotics, to make every sip healthy. Next is Vive Organic, the company’s brand of wellness shots, researched and crafted to give every drink a potent kick of natural ingredients. And finally, there is Slice, the company’s soda brand, offering a healthier soda choice without losing good taste.

All of this makes Suja Life a truly modern beverage company. The company’s products are available in more than 37,000 stores across the US. Suja Life prides itself on using ingredients that are organic, non-GMO, and plant-based.

Shares in SUJA hit the market on May 7, and the IPO closed the next day. Suja Life put 8,888,889 shares on the market, at an initial price of $21 each. The stock opened at $18 per share and closed at $17.85. The company sold 8,688,170 directly; the remaining 200,719 shares were sold by an exiting stockholder. Suja Life realized about $183 million in gross proceeds from the shares it sold directly.

Since its market debut, however, SUJA shares have largely moved in one direction – lower. The stock has declined about 18%, reflecting a combination of factors, including intense competition in the healthy beverage market, exposure to volatile fresh-fruit commodity costs, and investor concerns over the gap between revenue growth and profitability.

Attention now turns to the company’s first earnings report as a public company, scheduled for today. Wall Street expects first-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of ~$106 million.

Ahead of the print, Goldman Sachs’ Bonnie Herzog, an expert on the consumer staples retail sector, sees an attractive opportunity in this newly public company.

“We believe SUJA’s unique platform within the fast-growing NHB category positions the company well for strong top & bottom line growth over the next several years – supported in part by the company’s unique, vertically integrated supply chain, manufacturing facilities, and route-to-market that acts as a competitive advantage. Further, we see significant room for SUJA to continue to expand gross margins over the longer term – driven by topline leverage, a continued mix shift toward higher margin shots and strong existing relationships with suppliers that result in favorable supply agreements… Through this lens, and with the stock trading at just an 8.9x forward EV/EBITDA multiple – a 36% discount to Staples peers, we think valuation is very attractive,” Herzog stated.

With a Buy rating and a $31 price target, Herzog sees plenty of room for the shares to run, implying 112% upside from current levels. (To watch Herzog’s track record, click here)

All in all, this stock has picked up 5 analyst reviews in its short time as a public entity, with the 4 Buys and 1 Hold giving a Strong Buy consensus rating. With a share price of $14.64 and an average target price of $24.25, SUJA boasts a ~66% upside potential on the one-year horizon. (See SUJA stock forecast)

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analysts. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.

title: Paradise, Popped: Slice Soda Launches All-New Pineapple Flavor
content:
Available now at Albertsons, the latest addition to Slice's better-for-you lineup delivers bold sunny flavor, satisfying fizz and functional benefits in every sip

OCEANSIDE, Calif., June 3, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Slice Soda, the iconic soda brand reimagined for today's wellness-minded consumer and owned by Suja Life, Inc. (Nasdaq: SUJA), today announces the newest addition to its fizzy family of flavors: Pineapple, now available at Albertsons locations nationwide.Slice Soda's All-New Pineapple Flavor

Bright, crisp and refreshingly tropical, Slice Pineapple Soda delivers on both flavor and function, with the brand's signature combination of prebiotics, probiotics, and postbiotics to support gut health. Each can contains just 35 calories and 5 grams of sugar, with no high fructose corn syrup or artificial ingredients.

"Pineapple is basically a tropical getaway in a can," said Nicole Portwood, Chief Marketing Officer at Suja Life. "We wanted Pineapple to feel like an escape – bright, tropical and instantly mood-boosting – while still delivering on the better-for-you benefits that have become synonymous with Slice Soda."

Pineapple marks the latest addition to Slice's lineup, joining fizzy favorites such as Shirley Temple, Apple, Pacific Pop, Root Beer, Watermelon, Cherry Cola, Orange, Grape, Lemon Lime, Classic Cola, Cherry Cola, Watermelon, Ginger Ale, Grapefruit Spritz and Strawberry. Like all Slice products, Pineapple is non-GMO Project Verified, gluten-free, vegan, and contains no high fructose corn syrup. While most brands in the healthy soda category fall flat on the satisfying bubbles of traditional soda, Slice has the highest carbonation level in the category.

Slice Pineapple Soda is now available at Albertsons locations nationwide.

For more information, visit www.slicesoda.com or follow @slicesoda on Instagram.

About Slice Soda
Slice Soda is the classic soda you know and love, now made better. Born in 1984 as a mainstream soda, Slice was reimagined and reformulated by Suja Life, a leader in healthy beverages, for today's consumer. A leveled-up version of the original that tastes like pure nostalgia, Slice is boldly carbonated, full-flavored soda that contains a unique blend of prebiotics, probiotics, and postbiotics. Available in a variety of flavors from citrusy favorites like Orange and Lemon Lime to iconic Grape and Classic Cola, each can is 40 calories or less with five grams of sugar or less, and never any high fructose corn syrup. Slice is non-GMO Project verified, gluten-free, vegan, and kosher. For more information on Slice, including where to purchase it, please visit www.slicesoda.com and follow @slicesoda on Instagram.

Story Continues

About Suja Life
At Suja Life, we're changing what beverages bring to the table. We make organic, cold-pressed juices, wellness shots, and better-for-you sodas that deliver real functional benefits, exceptional taste, and high-quality ingredients, because we believe beverages should be as delicious as they are good for you. Our three brands – Suja Organic, Vive Organic, and Slice Soda – reach consumers through thousands of retail doors nationally. We operate a vertically integrated high-pressure processing and cold-pressed beverage facility, processing approximately 1 million pounds of organic produce each week and moving from farm to bottle in as few as eight days. With category-leading brands, a dedication to operational excellence, and a proven innovation engine, Suja Life is positioned at the front of the growing natural healthy beverage space.Slice Soda (PRNewsfoto/Slice Soda)Cision

title: Catalyst Watch: FedEx splits, tech CEOs hit Computex, Quantinuum IPO, and the jobs report
content:
[Wall Street New York stock exchange stock market]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JUNE 1

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) and Globalstar (GSAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSAT]) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Momentus (MNTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNTS]), Hyliion (HYLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HYLN]), and Redwire (RDW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDW]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verra Mobility (VRRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRRM]) and Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]). Short interest is elevated on Kohl's (KSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KSS]) after its +20% earnings move. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPE]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), Halliburton (HAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAL]), Cigna (CI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CI]), and Western Digital (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]) headlines a busy week for IPOs. The Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON])-backed quantum computing platform is looking to raise up to $1.05B and to value it at roughly $12.7B. Other IPOs expected to price and begin trading include aerospace components manufacturer Applied Aerospace & Defense (AADX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AADX]), silver miner Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining (GATO), Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]), coastal property insurer Safepoint Holdings (SFPT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SFPT]), and gas energy firm Innio Holding (INIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INIO]). The analyst quiet period ends on Hawkeye 360 (HAWK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAWK]), Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]), Rare Earths Americas (REA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REA]), and Odyssey Therapeutics (ODTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODTX]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* DATA WATCH - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on monthly deliveries from Nio (NIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIO]), XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]), Li Auto (LI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LI]), and BYD Company (BYDDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDF]). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
 	* ALL DAY - FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) will officially split off its less-than-truckload trucking arm, FedEx Freight (FDXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDXF]), into a separate, publicly traded company while keeping the express and ground parcel businesses inside FedEx Corp. The move is structured as a tax-efficient spin-off. FedEx Freight will be a component of the S&P 500 Index right out of the gate.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Computex Taipei event will begin. The AI-first show includes a wall-to-wall lineup of chip CEOs, heavyweight forums on physical AI, and a dense calendar of launches spanning PCs, data centers, edge, and robotics. Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) CEO Cristiano Amon, Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Arm (ARM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARM]) CEO Rene Haas, and Marvell Technologies (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]) CEO Matt Murphy will all give keynotes or major presentations. Nvidia is effectively co-branding its Computex presence as "NVIDIA GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX," running across the week of the show and CEO Jensen Huang delivers a high-profile keynote. Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]) also plans a big presence.
 	* ALL DAY - Snowflake Summit 26 next week will begin in San Francisco. Snowflake's (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]) four-day flagship conference in San Francisco will be focused on "Making AI Real for Business," with heavy emphasis on enterprise data, generative/agentic AI, and applications.
 	* ALL DAY - The Atlantic hurricane season officially begins. Traders expect more volatility than normal around crude oil futures this year if any major hurricanes develop near offshore drilling assets.

TUESDAY - JUNE 2

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PANW]), Dollar General (DG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DG]), Ulta Beauty (ULTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ULTA]), Signet Jewelers (SIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIG]), and Yesway (YSWY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YSWY]), which will be reporting for the first time as a public company. Options trading implies double-digit swings for Oddity Tech (ODD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODD]) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSCO]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the 2026 Bank of Korea International Conference.
 	* ALL DAY - The Microsoft Build event will begin in San Francisco. The flagship developer showcase will kick off with a keynote from Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) CEO Satya Nadella.
 	* ALL DAY - The two-day Deutsche Bank Basic Materials Conference will begin. The conference will include a long list of presentations from senior executives across chemicals, mining, industrials, and related basic materials companies. 
 	* 8:30 A.M. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will give remarks and participate in a conversation on monetary policy.

WEDNESDAY - JUNE 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), Crowdstrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), Five Below (FIVE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIVE]), Medtronic (MDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDT]), and Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Netskope (NTSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTSK]) and Petco Health and Wellness (WOOF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOOF]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]) will hold an Aerospace Investor Day event ahead of the planned spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace later in the year.
 	* 3:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will release its May sales report.

THURSDAY - JUNE 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Ciena (CIEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CIEN]), Samsara (IOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IOT]), Rubrik (RBRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBRK]), Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for X-Energy (XE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XE]) and Planet Labs (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Radian Group's (RDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDN]) Investor Day, Solstice Advanced Materials' (SOLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOLS]) business update call, and Lucid Group's (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]) annual meeting.
 	* ALL DAY - Boralex (BRLXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BRLXF]) (BLX:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLX:CA]) shareholders will vote on the acquisition offer from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAM]) (BEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEP]) (BN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BN]) and Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec.

FRIDAY - JUNE 5

 	* ALL DAY - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the one-year anniversary date of the Circle (CRCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRCL]) IPO.
 	* ALL DAY - The live-action _Masters of the Universe_ movie from Amazon MGM Studios and Mattel Studios is set to hit U.S. theaters. Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) has a significant merchandising program tied to the film.
 	* ALL DAY - The Summer Game Fest conference will take place. The video game conference could generate buzz that is relevant to Sony's (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]) PlayStation, Nintendo (NTDOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTDOY]), Microsoft's (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) Xbox, and major third-party publishers like Capcom, Square Enix, Bandai Namco, CD Projekt, and Epic Games.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The May jobs report will be released. Economists expect 93K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.3%.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

MORE ON THE MARKETS

 	* Blinded By Greed? [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4910001-blinded-by-greed]

title: Suja Life Announces Pricing of Initial Public Offering
content:
OCEANSIDE, Calif., May  07, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Suja Life, Inc. today announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 8,888,889 shares of its Class A common stock (“Class A Common Stock”) which are being offered by Suja Life as described in the registration statement on Form S-1 filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”), at a public offering price of $21.00 per share. Suja Life has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,333,333 shares of Class A Common Stock at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discounts and commissions.

The shares are expected to begin trading on The Nasdaq Global Select Market on May 7, 2026 under the ticker symbol “SUJA.” The offering is expected to close on May 8, 2026 subject to customary closing conditions.

Upon completion of the initial public offering, Suja Life will be the sole general partner of Suja Life Holdings, L.P. (“Holdings LP”) and will exclusively operate and control all of its business and affairs.

Suja Life will receive net proceeds of approximately $173.6 million after deducting the underwriting discounts and commissions but before estimated expenses and intends to use the net proceeds received from this offering to acquire, directly or indirectly through one or more wholly owned subsidiaries newly issued LP units in Holdings LP at a purchase price per LP unit equal to the initial public offering price per share of Class A Common Stock, less the underwriting discounts and commissions.

Holdings LP will, in turn, use the balance of the net proceeds it receives from Suja Life (i) to repay $141.3 million of outstanding borrowings under its credit agreement, (ii) to pay approximately $17.5 million in cash payments to certain employees and directors of Holdings LP in connection with the settlement of time-based vesting incentive units, in partial satisfaction of certain transaction bonus agreements and in connection with celebratory cash awards in respect of the closing of this offering and (iii) to pay expenses incurred in connection with this offering and the related organizational transactions.

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Jefferies, and William Blair are acting as joint lead bookrunning managers for the proposed offering. BofA Securities and Evercore ISI are acting as bookrunning managers for the proposed offering.

A registration statement relating to these securities was declared effective by the SEC on May 6, 2026. This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy these securities, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

Copies of the final prospectus related to this offering, when available, may be obtained from: Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Attention: Prospectus Department, 200 West Street, New York, NY 10282, or by telephone at (866) 471-2526, or by email at prospectus-ny@ny.email.gs.com; or Jefferies LLC, Attention: Equity Syndicate Prospectus Department, 520 Madison Avenue, New York, New York 10022, by telephone at 1-877-821-7388, or by email at prospectus_department@jefferies.com; or William Blair & Company, L.L.C., Attn: Prospectus Department, 150 North Riverside Plaza, Chicago, Illinois 60606, by telephone at 1-800-621-0687 or by email at: prospectus@williamblair.com.

About Suja Life
At Suja Life, we're changing what beverages bring to the table. We make organic, cold-pressed juices, wellness shots, and better-for-you sodas that deliver real functional benefits, exceptional taste, and high-quality ingredients, because we believe beverages should be as delicious as they are good for you. Our three brands – Suja Organic, Vive Organic, and Slice Soda – reach consumers through thousands of retail doors nationally. We operate a vertically integrated high-pressure processing and cold-pressed beverage facility, processing approximately 1 million pounds of organic produce each week and moving from farm to bottle in as few as eight days. With category-leading brands, a dedication to operational excellence, and a proven innovation engine, Suja Life is positioned at the front of the growing natural healthy beverage space.

Contact:
ICR, Inc.
sujalife@icrinc.com

title: Suja Life Announces Pricing of Initial Public Offering

LLC, Attention: Prospectus Department, 200 West Street, New York, NY 10282, or by telephone at (866) 471-2526, or by email at prospectus-ny@ny.email.gs.com; or Jefferies LLC, Attention: Equity Syndicate Prospectus Department, 520 Madison Avenue, New York, New York 10022, by telephone at 1-877-821-7388, or by email at prospectus_department@jefferies.com; or William Blair & Company, L.L.C., Attn: Prospectus Department, 150 North Riverside Plaza, Chicago, Illinois 60606, by telephone at 1-800-621-0687 or by email at: prospectus@williamblair.com.

About Suja Life
At Suja Life, we're changing what beverages bring to the table. We make organic, cold-pressed juices, wellness shots, and better-for-you sodas that deliver real functional benefits, exceptional taste, and high-quality ingredients, because we believe beverages should be as delicious as they are good for you. Our three brands – Suja Organic, Vive Organic, and Slice Soda – reach consumers through thousands of retail doors nationally. We operate a vertically integrated high-pressure processing and cold-pressed beverage facility, processing approximately 1 million pounds of organic produce each week and moving from farm to bottle in as few as eight days. With category-leading brands, a dedication to operational excellence, and a proven innovation engine, Suja Life is positioned at the front of the growing natural healthy beverage space.

title: Suja Life Announces Filing of Registration Statement for Proposed Initial Public Offering
content:
Suja Life, Inc.

OCEANSIDE, Calif., April 10, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Suja Life, Inc. today announced that it has filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") relating to the proposed initial public offering of shares of its common stock. The number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined. The proposed offering is subject to market and other conditions, and there can be no assurance as to whether or when the offering may be completed, or as to the actual size or terms of the offering. Suja Life, Inc. has applied to list its common stock on The Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol "SUJA."

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Jefferies, and William Blair are acting as joint lead bookrunning managers for the proposed offering. BofA Securities and Evercore ISI are acting as bookrunning managers for the proposed offering.

The proposed offering will be made only by means of a prospectus. A copy of the preliminary prospectus, when available, may be obtained from Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Attention: Prospectus Department, 200 West Street, New York, NY 10282, or by telephone at (866) 471-2526, or by email at prospectus-ny@ny.email.gs.com; or Jefferies LLC, Attention: Equity Syndicate Prospectus Department, 520 Madison Avenue, New York, New York 10022, by telephone at 1-877-821-7388, or by email at prospectus_department@jefferies.com; or William Blair & Company, L.L.C., Attn: Prospectus Department, 150 North Riverside Plaza, Chicago, Illinois 60606, by telephone at 1-800-621-0687 or by email at: prospectus@williamblair.com.

A registration statement relating to the proposed sale of these securities has been filed with the SEC but has not yet become effective. These securities may not be sold, nor may offers to buy be accepted, prior to the time the registration statement becomes effective. This release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About Suja Life
At Suja Life, we're changing what beverages bring to the table. We make organic, cold-pressed juices, wellness shots, and better-for-you sodas that deliver real functional benefits, exceptional taste, and high-quality ingredients, because we believe beverages should be as delicious as they are good for you. Our three brands – Suja Organic, Vive Organic, and Slice Soda – reach consumers through thousands of retail doors nationally. We operate a vertically integrated high-pressure processing and cold-pressed beverage facility, processing approximately 1 million pounds of organic produce each week and moving from farm to bottle in as few as eight days. With category-leading brands, a dedication to operational excellence, and a proven innovation engine, Suja Life is positioned at the front of the growing natural healthy beverage space.
